commands.review_summary:
This PR moves MSTeams conversation references and poll metadata/votes from JSON file stores to plugin-state SQLite, adds one-shot legacy imports, and updates callers and tests.

PR surface: Source +426, Tests +353. Total +779 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor PR, not a reported current-main bug. The relevant check is source and test review of storage migration behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is indicated; this protected-label XL persistence migration needs maintainer acceptance of the upgrade risk and proof gap.

target.title: refactor(msteams): persist conversation and poll stores in sqlite
target.body:
Summary
- Move MSTeams proactive conversation references from the JSON file store into plugin-state SQLite with one-shot legacy import and timestamp-compatible pruning.
- Move MSTeams polls into plugin-state SQLite, splitting poll metadata and sharded vote buckets so large polls do not store unbounded vote maps in one row.
- Keep SSO/delegated token storage unchanged; update callers/tests to use the new state-backed stores and keep test-only runtime helpers out of package source.

Verification
- pnpm test extensions/msteams/src/conversation-store.shared.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/conversation-store-state.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/polls.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/pending-uploads-fs.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/send-context.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages.read.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/graph-group-management.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/outbound.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor.lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
- /Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Review MSTeams conversation and poll store move to plugin-state SQLite after combined in-process/file-backed locks, registerIfAbsent metadata imports, timestamp cap pruning, read paths that do not delete expired rows, poll metadata/vote buckets without independent SQLite TTL, sharded bounded vote buckets, idempotent legacy vote bucket fill where existing SQLite voter choices win, and test runtime moved under excluded test-support. Confirm SSO/delegated tokens are untouched, legacy JSON import is one-shot and cannot overwrite existing SQLite rows or skip/overwrite votes after partial import, runtime uses getMSTeamsRuntime().state.openKeyedStore rather than direct core store imports, concurrent poll votes/conversation upserts preserve existing behavior across same-process and cross-process writers, cap eviction preserves lastSeenAt/updatedAt semantics, large polls do not hit plugin-state value/row caps under intended bounds, and proactive send/poll behavior is preserved.'
- node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed" (provider: blacksmith-testbox, id: tbx_01ksx1tgap2kb1109qvwd1m2vc, Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691400428)

Behavior addressed: MSTeams conversation and poll plugin-local state now persists through per-agent plugin-state SQLite instead of auxiliary JSON files.
Real environment tested: Local macOS focused MSTeams Vitest plus delegated Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox for changed checks.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final patch.
Evidence after fix: Focused MSTeams tests passed 10 files / 102 tests; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Testbox check:changed exited 0.
Observed result after fix: Conversation references, poll metadata, and poll votes read/write through plugin runtime SQLite state; legacy JSON imports preserve existing SQLite rows and fill missing vote buckets without overwriting current votes.
What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams webhook traffic against a real tenant was not exercised in this PR.
target.author: steipete
